concerns.text: Task T017a (Data Generation) is marked as dependent on T016 (SequentialSinkhornSolver), but T016 is listed AFTER T017a in the task list. The producer (T016) must precede the consumer (T017a) to satisfy the data-flow dependency for generating ground-truth labels.

Task T025 (Sensitivity Analysis) depends on T030b (30 simulation runs), but T025 is listed BEFORE T030b in the task list. The consumer (T025) cannot execute before the producer (T030b) generates the required simulation artifacts.

Task T031 (t-test) depends on T030b (30 runs) and T032 (theoretical lower bound), but T031 is listed BEFORE T032. The theoretical lower bound (T032) must be computed before the t-test (T031) can compare results against it.
Task T001d (Execute checksumming) depends on T001c (Implement checksumming script), but T001d is listed BEFORE T001c. The script must be implemented before it can be executed.

Task T010 (Unit test for moment extraction) and T011 (Unit test for Sinkhorn) are listed in Phase 2 but depend on T005 (epsilon utilities) and T016 (Sinkhorn solver) respectively. While T005 is present, T016 is listed after these tests, violating the 'test before implementation' or 'implementation before test' ordering depending on interpretation, but strictly T016 must exist for T011 to run.

Task T017b (Ground-truth label computation) is listed after T017a (Matrix generation) but logically requires the Sinkhorn solver (T016) which is listed later. The ordering of T017a/b relative to T016 is ambiguous and risks execution failure if T016 is not completed first.
